perturbation.original_text: “ Restricted Business ” shall mean the business of providing a skin substitute for chronic wound care.
perturbation.changed_text: “ Restricted Business ” shall mean the business of providing a skin substitute for acute wound care.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the definition of Restricted Business from chronic wound care to acute wound care. This contradicts the implication and focus of the original contract, which is on chronic wound care, creating uncertainty as to what constitutes a violation of the non-compete agreement. Specifically, later references to the Company's TheraSkin® product (used for chronic wounds) now conflict with this altered definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) During the Restricted Period, the Restricted Party shall not, directly or indirectly: (i) solicit business from, interfere with, attempt to solicit business from, interfere with, or do business with any actual or prospective customer or client with whom the Company or the Alliqua Entities does or has done business (including direct purchasers of the Company’s TheraSkin® product), in any case for the purpose of discussing or securing business or contracts related to the Restricted Business, or (ii) attempt to influence, encourage, persuade or induce any such actual or prospective customer or client whom the Company or the Alliqua Entities does or has done business to reduce the extent of its business dealings with the Alliqua Entities (including direct purchasers of the Company’s TheraSkin® product).
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Restricted Business" from chronic wound care to acute wound care. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifically mentions "TheraSkin® product," a product used for chronic wounds. Since the Restricted Business is now defined as acute wound care, soliciting business related to TheraSkin® (chronic wound care) might not be considered a violation of the non-compete agreement, creating a significant conflict and making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding chronic wound care products, specifically the Company's TheraSkin® product.

perturbation.original_text: “ Restricted Period ” shall mean a period commencing from the Effective Date until the two (2)-year anniversary of the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: “ Restricted Period ” shall mean a period commencing from the Effective Date until the five (5)-year anniversary of the Effective Date; however, if the Restricted Party achieves certain sales targets, the Restricted Period reduces to one (1) year.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the Restricted Period introduces two contradictions. First, it extends the non-compete from two years to five, conflicting with the initial agreement. Second, it introduces a conditional reduction to one year based on sales targets, creating ambiguity and potentially incentivizing the Restricted Party to manipulate or disclose sales data to shorten the period, thus working against Alliqua's interests. This contradicts the fundamental intent of the non-compete, which is to protect Alliqua's business for a fixed period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) This Agreement is entered into in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Contribution and Merger Agreement. The parties acknowledge that it would be detrimental to the Alliqua Entities if the Restricted Party were to compete with the Alliqua Entities any time during the Restricted Period or to solicit customers, clients, employees or contractors of the Company or Alliqua Entities at any time during the Restricted Period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the "Restricted Period" from a fixed two-year term to a variable term that could be as long as five years or as short as one year, depending on sales targets. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that it would be detrimental to Alliqua if the Restricted Party competed with them "any time during the Restricted Period." The original text provided a clear, fixed Restricted Period, whereas the changed text introduces ambiguity and a conditional element that undermines the purpose of the non-compete agreement described in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. Specifically, the change makes the duration of the non-compete unclear and creates a conflict with the intention of the clause in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Restricted Party agrees to preserve and protect the confidentiality of all Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Restricted Party agrees to preserve and protect the confidentiality of all Confidential Information, except as required for securing new employment.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to the confidentiality obligation, allowing the Restricted Party to disclose Confidential Information under the guise of securing new employment. This directly contradicts the core purpose of the non-disclosure clause and creates a loophole that undermines Alliqua's protection of its proprietary information. Section 4 starts with an agreement to protect all confidential information, so the new text creating an exception to the Confidential Information is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Non-Disclosure . The Restricted Party agrees not to dispute, contest, or deny any ownership rights of the Alliqua Entities of the Confidential Information or the Company Intellectual Property. The Restricted Party agrees to preserve and protect the confidentiality of all Confidential Information. The Restricted Party shall not directly or indirectly, disclose to any unauthorized person or use for the Restricted Party’s own account any Confidential Information. The Restricted Party shall hold all Confidential Information in the strictest confidence, and take all commercially reasonable precautions to prevent its inadvertent disclosure to any unauthorized person. The Restricted Party shall not, directly or indirectly, other than on behalf of the Alliqua Entities, utilize, disclose or make available to any other person or entity, any of the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, the covenants in this Section 4 shall not apply to Confidential Information that the Restricted Party is required to disclose by applicable Law, after providing written notice to the Alliqua Entities before producing the information, which notice shall be given as soon as practicable, so that the Alliqua Entities, at their sole expense, may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'The Restricted Party agrees to preserve and protect the confidentiality of all Confidential Information.' The changed text adds an exception: 'except as required for securing new employment.' This new exception directly contradicts the non-disclosure agreement stated in the contradicted text, especially since the contradicted text explicitly states 'The Restricted Party agrees to preserve and protect the confidentiality of all Confidential Information' without exception. The change allows for disclosure, while the original clause prohibits it, creating a strong in-text contradiction, as per the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: During the Restricted Period, the Restricted Party shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce, recruit or encourage anyone who is a consultant or employee of the Company or the Alliqua Entities to terminate his or her employment or consulting relationship with the Company or the Alliqua Entities, or without the consent of the Alliqua Entities, hire, retain or engage any such person for the Restricted Party or for another Person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Restricted Period, the Restricted Party may, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce, recruit or encourage anyone who is a consultant or employee of the Company or the Alliqua Entities to terminate his or her employment or consulting relationship with the Company or the Alliqua Entities, or without the consent of the Alliqua Entities, hire, retain or engage any such person for the Restricted Party or for another Person or entity if it does not amount to more than 5 employees.
perturbation.explanation: This insertion now allows the Restricted Party to solicit and recruit up to five employees, directly contradicting the non-solicitation clause and creating a clear conflict regarding employee poaching. The original text forbade any solicitation, but the modified text now permits it to a limited extent, undermining the protection of Alliqua's workforce. The change directly contradicts the start of the 5(b) clause that forbits such actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) During the Restricted Period, the Restricted Party shall not, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce, recruit or encourage anyone who is a consultant or employee of the Company or the Alliqua Entities to terminate his or her employment or consulting relationship with the Company or the Alliqua Entities, or without the consent of the Alliqua Entities, hire, retain or engage any such person for the Restricted Party or for another Person or entity. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, nothing in this Section 5(b) shall prevent the Restricted Party or any of its Affiliates from hiring, soliciting, inducing, recruiting or encouraging (i) any employee or consultant whose employment or consultancy has been terminated by the Company or the Alliqua Entities; or (ii) twelve (12) months after the date of termination of employment, any employee or consultant whose employment has been terminated by the employee or the consultant.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text explicitly prohibit the Restricted Party from soliciting employees of the Company or Alliqua Entities. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing the Restricted Party to solicit up to five employees. This directly conflicts with the original prohibition, creating a clear in-text contradiction as it undermines the non-solicitation clause found in the contradicted section, 5(b), by now permitting solicitation activities previously forbidden.

perturbation.original_text: If the Restricted Party violates any of the restrictions contained in this Article 5 , the Restricted Period shall be suspended and shall not run in favor of the Restricted Party from the time of commencement of any violation until the time when the Restricted Party ceases the violation.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Restricted Party violates any of the restrictions contained in this Article 5, the Restricted Period shall continue uninterrupted. No violation of the non-compete terms will extend the original Restrictive Period.
perturbation.explanation: The original language suspends the Restricted Period in case of violation, punishing the violator by extending the non-compete. The modified language completely negates this, stating that the period continues uninterrupted regardless of any violations. This creates a massive disincentive for compliance and contradicts the purpose of the non-compete agreement, making it less enforceable. The change creates an in-text contradiction by negating the very first sentence of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (e) If the Restricted Party violates any of the restrictions contained in this Article 5 , the Restricted Period shall be suspended and shall not run in favor of the Restricted Party from the time of commencement of any violation until the time when the Restricted Party ceases the violation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text both state that violations of Article 5 will suspend the Restricted Period. However, the changed text states that the Restricted Period will continue uninterrupted, regardless of violations. This creates a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Therefore, the Restricted Party agrees that the Alliqua Entities shall be entitled to seek a temporary restraining order and injunctive relief restraining the Restricted Party from the commission of any breach or threatened breach of any covenants herein, without the necessity of establishing irreparable harm or the posting of a bond, and to recover from the Restricted Party damages incurred, as well as the attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses related to any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and enforcement of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Therefore, the Restricted Party agrees that the Alliqua Entities shall be entitled to seek monetary compensation only up to $500. No temporary restraining order and injunctive relief restraining will be sought by the Alliqua Entities from the commission of any breach or threatened breach of any covenants herein.
perturbation.explanation: This clause initially allows Alliqua to seek injunctive relief and damages. The modified clause restricts Alliqua to seeking only $500 in monetary compensation and explicitly prohibits seeking injunctive relief. This undermines Alliqua's ability to effectively enforce the agreement and contradicts the original intent of providing strong remedies for breach, significantly impacting its enforceability. This also contradicts the opening statement of the Remedies section in Paragraph 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies . The Restricted Party acknowledges that the restrictions contained herein, in view of the nature of the Company’s business and the businesses of the Alliqua Entities, are reasonable and necessary to protect their legitimate business interests, business goodwill and reputation, and that any violation of these restrictions would result in irreparable injury and continuing damage to them, and that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Therefore, the Restricted Party agrees that the Alliqua Entities shall be entitled to seek a temporary restraining order and injunctive relief restraining the Restricted Party from the commission of any breach or threatened breach of any covenants herein, without the necessity of establishing irreparable harm or the posting of a bond, and to recover from the Restricted Party damages incurred, as well as the attorneys’ fees, costs and expenses related to any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement and enforcement of this Agreement. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as prohibiting the Alliqua Entities from pursuing any other remedies available to them for any breach or threatened breach, including, without limitation, the recovery of money damages, attorneys’ fees, and costs. The existence of any claim or cause of action by the Restricted Party, whether predicated on this Agreement or otherwise, shall not constitute a defense to the enforcement of the restrictive covenants contained herein, or preclude injunctive relief.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text (which are identical in this case) state that Alliqua is entitled to seek injunctive relief and damages for any breach. The changed text restricts Alliqua to seeking only $500 in monetary compensation and explicitly prohibits seeking injunctive relief. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text removes the option for injunctive relief, which the contradicted text explicitly allows, thus creating a structural flaw and a clear contradiction. This falls directly under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
